title: Ptolemy's World Map
memoir: At the start of the second century CE, four great empires ruled over the ancient world. In the East, the Han Empire consolidated its power and expanded into central Asia, exporting its precious commodities along the Silk Road. In India, the Kushan Empire was advancing further into Subcontinent, creating a unique blend of Hellenistic, Indian, and Persian cultures. In the West, the Roman Empire was at its apex. The legions pushed the borders forward while the Empire enjoyed a long period of peace and prosperity - the Pax Romana. Parthia, too, held fast, allowing the Romans only limited gains, and keeping the monopoly over trade routes linking the East with the Mediterranean. Truly, this was the “Age of Empires,” an unprecedented period of optimism, inquiry, and growth for all four great powers that ruled over the vast territories of Eurasia. It was a fertile ground for the exchange of people, ideas, and knowledge. Thus, it is unsurprising that one of the most famous maps of antiquity, but also history in general - Ptolemy's world map — was made at this time.
